_comment: CSS variable → Tailwind utility translation hints. Used by porting agents and by the migration handbook. Keys are the CSS custom-property names found in src/css/wh40k-rpg.css; values are the recommended Tailwind utility (without the tw- prefix; templates author with the prefix). Anything not in this map should fall through to a hand-decision documented in docs/tailwind-migration.md. Source-of-truth for tokens is tailwind.config.js — this file is a translation cheat sheet.
_color_notes: Each color token can substitute the leading `text-` prefix for `bg-`, `border-`, `ring-`, etc. depending on context.

animations._note: Keyframes that survive into Tailwind go in tailwind.config.js theme.extend.keyframes + theme.extend.animation. Two are migrated already; agents porting templates that depend on a non-migrated keyframe must (1) port the keyframe to tailwind.config.js, (2) use the `animate-<name>` utility on the template, (3) delete the original @keyframes block from the monolith.

_apply_policy: Do NOT use @apply in newly authored CSS or in component plugins. The 20 @apply uses in the monolith are technical debt — when a template that consumes one is ported, inline the underlying utilities at the call site and delete the @apply rule. The only case for using @apply is inside `tailwind.config.js` `addComponents()` — and even there, prefer raw CSS over @apply because the prefix configuration leaks badly.
_foundry_chrome_exceptions: These selectors target Foundry's V14 chrome (chat, ProseMirror, app windows) and CANNOT be ported to per-template Tailwind. They live in the residual CSS layer permanently: .ProseMirror, .editor-content, #chat-log, #chat-controls, .app, .application, .window-content, .form-fields, .filepicker. When deleting source-blocks, keep these selectors — move them into a small `src/css/foundry-chrome.css` and import that into the monolith head, then delete the rest of the block.
